observations.recommendation:
Based on the AAR foregoing, we recommended that the management direct the following officials to the subsequent courses of action:
Municipal Accountant:
a) Prepare the Journal Voucher (JV) to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card (Annex B), respectively;
b) Support the JV with the Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads, working papers on the distribution of costs for the road components, and working papers on the determination of the depreciated replacement cost for road components with no available cost per registry;
c) Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and
d) Include disclosures of the Local Road Network System in the Notes to Financial Statements in the aforementioned prescribed format.

Municipal General Services Officer:
a) Maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map;
b) Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and their components (Annex C); and
c) Prepare the Report on the Local Road Network of the Municipal Local Government Unit (MLGU) of Alamada (Annex D).

Municipal Engineer:
Provide the Municipal Accountant and the GSO with a complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

observations.recommendation:
Based on the foregoing, we recommended that the management direct the following
officials to the subsequent courses of action:

a) For the Municipal Accountant to conduct a thorough review of capitalized
expenditures recorded under Infrastructure Assets – Road Networks and
prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify routine maintenance
expenses as Repairs and Maintenance expenses;
b) For the General Services Officer to coordinate closely with the Municipal
Engineering Office (MEO) to expedite the revision of the Local Road Map
and ensure that all required key information is complete and fully
compliant with the standards set under COA Circular No. 2015-008; and
c) For the Municipal Mayor to issue an updated memorandum
reconstituting the Road Inventory Committee with active personnel from
relevant departments, ensuring that it aligns with the requirements of
COA Circular No. 2015-008 and supports accurate financial reporting and
infrastructure management.

Based on the foregoing observations, we recommended that the management
direct the following:

Municipal Accountant:
Prepare journal entry/ies to transfer the completed PPE from the CIP accounts to
the corresponding PPE accounts as soon as it is completed and available for use
in order to ensure the proper recognition and recording of assets.

General Services Officer:
Properly conduct an annual physical inventory of all supplies and properties of the
local government unit as of December 31 of each year as mandated by the pertinent
regulations. The process shall give consideration to the items found at the station
or items not included in the inventory working papers, but there is a reasonable
basis to consider the same as owned by the agency and non-existing/missing PPEs
per physical count to come up with the reconciled balances of PPE accounts to be
considered as the correct balance of the agency's PPEs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:

a. The Municipal Accountant to record all consummated procurement of
supplies and materials through the inventory account based on the
Acceptance and Inspection Report together with the supporting documents
and all issuances through the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued
with the original Requisition and Issue Slip, and to properly follow the steps in
recording the receipt and issuance of supplies and materials using the perpetual
inventory method;
b. The Municipal Accountant and the General Service Officer to maintain
Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards, respectively, as required in Sections
119 and 120 of the NGAS Manual Volume I for local government units; and
c. The General Service Officer to conduct a physical inventory of supplies
and materials at every end of the semester and submit a Report on the
Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to the Municipal Accountant for
reconciliation with the records, and to the Audit Team.

In light of the foregoing observations, we recommended the following courses of
actions:

1) The Municipal Accountant review and reclassify all semi-expendable
properties erroneously recorded and use the appropriate semi-
expendable property accounts, in accordance with COA Circular No.
2024-006;
2) Perform a thorough reconciliation of all semi-expendable property
acquisitions for CY 2024 and prior years to identify and correct any
remaining unclassified items that may have been inadvertently left out
during initial reclassification;
3) Ensure that Property Cards, Ledger Cards, Registry of Semi-Expendable
Property Issued, and Report of Semi-Expendable Property Issued are
maintained for all semi-expendable properties, regardless of immediate
issuance, to ensure proper documentation and monitoring; and,
4) Conduct a physical inventory of all semi-expendable properties and
submit the required Report on the Physical Count of Semi-Expendable
Property (RPCSP) in a timely manner to comply with existing accounting
and auditing requirements.

In line of the foregoing, we recommended the following courses of actions:

a) Maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through full implementation of
GAD-related programs and projects in order to attain the objective for
which funds were provided;
b) Create/assign a Responsibility Center (RC) for their GAD Focal Point
System (GFPS) as mandated in COA Circular 2021-008 dated September
6, 2021;
c) Strictly follow the prescribed procedures for the submission of the
Annual GAD Plan and Budget (GPB), as well as the GAD Accomplishment
Report (AR), to the Commission on Audit (COA) to ensure transparency
and compliance with the audit requirements for GAD funds; and
d) Implement stronger internal control mechanisms, especially in financial
transactions related to GAD programs, such as requiring clear and
verifiable documentation for any financial disbursements, and regular
monitoring and audits be conducted to mitigate risks of misappropriation
or misuse of funds.

We recommended to the management the following courses of actions:

a. Ensure full compliance with the reporting requirements set forth in
DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2017. This includes the
timely preparation and submission of both quarterly and annual reports
to all concerned agencies, as mandated; and
b. To prevent similar lapses in the future, management may consider
establishing a monitoring system or assigning a designated officer
responsible for tracking and ensuring compliance with reporting
obligations.

Based on the AAR foregoing, we recommended that the management direct the following officials to the subsequent courses of action:
a) Make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the error and ensure the balances reflected in the financial statements are presented fairly;
b) Refrain from delayed recording of transactions to avoid inaccuracies and omissions; and
c) Ensure proper use of account titles in recording transactions.

Based on the AAR foregoing, we recommended the management the following courses of actions:
a) Direct the Local Finance Committee, as well as the Municipal Planning Development Coordinator (MPDC), to reevaluate the current programs, projects and activities (PPAS) funded under the 20% DF, and recommend the reversion or rectification of the ineligible PPAs, if possible, to the appropriate funds;
b) Require the Municipal Budget Officer to review and thoroughly evaluate the expenditure items included in the budget proposals, specifically under the 20% DF, to prevent the disbursement of disallowable items, such as those mentioned in item 3.2.5 of JMC No. 1; and,
c) Ensure that said JMC No. 1 has been properly interpreted and resolve doubts by conducting inquiries or seeking clarifications with the appropriate governing bodies concerning the provisions in question in the said Circular.

Based on the AAR foregoing observations, we recommended the management direct the following officers to: 1) Ensure that the PPAs charged from the prior years (PY) unexpended balances lodged in the Special Trust Fund are duly indicated in the Annual Investment Plan or any Supplemental Investment Plan prior to the release and utilization thereof; and 2) Ensure that the preparation of the LDRRMFIP conforms with the provisions of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and includes the projects and activities to be sourced from the previous year’s unexpended balances;

The MPDC:
1) Coordinate with the MDRRMO and prepare the necessary revised AIP in accordance with the provisions stated in COA Circular 2012-002, and RA 10121.

We also recommended that the management adhere to the provisions of the DILG Memorandum Circular 2022-105 and DBM Budget Circular No. 2022-1 and stop the charging of motor vehicles, not exclusively used for response and rescue operations to the LDRRMF.

In line with the AAR foregoing, we recommended to the management the following actions:
a) Propose and enact a Septage and Sewerage Management Ordinance (SSMO) of the Municipality;
b) Ensure full compliance with the policy and guidelines stipulated in DILG Memorandum Circular 2019-62 dated April 22, 2019, and compliance with the provisions of the Philippine Clean Water Act of 2004; and
c) Prioritize projects that can address pollution prevention, control, and abatement in the water resources.

a) Propose and enact a Septage and Sewerage Management Ordinance (SSMO) of

Management’s comments:
The Management ensured to submit a request for the creation of an ordinance pertaining

Monitoring and Auditing Report (SCMAR) to Environment Management Bureau to ensure

Based on the foregoing observations, the audit team recommended that the management direct
the following officers to:

The Municipal Accountant:
1) Manually monitor the system-generated PPE ledgers and reconcile the same with the
RPCPPE submitted by the GSO;
2) Coordinate with the person-in-charge of the e-NGAS system and lodge the query
pertaining to the system error, forward any communication status with the audit team for
record purposes, and update the record and the system accordingly as soon as the
glitch is resolved;
3) Compute or trace any discrepancy with the RPCPPE and prepare proper adjusting
entries to correct the same; and,
4) Ensure that the accounting procedures were done in a timely manner, through proper
time management and sound internal control, in order to achieve a fair presentation of
financial statements.

The General Services Officer:
Prepare the IIRUP as directed by MNGAS for LGU, Section 79 of PD 1445, and Section 379 of
RA 7160, and facilitate the disposal of any unserviceable properties determined therein.

The Audit team also recommended for the management to direct the Inventory Committee
facilitating the “One Time Cleansing” operation of the Municipality to expedite the process since
they are already a year behind schedule as stated in their Physical Inventory Plan (PIP).

In view of the foregoing observations, the Audit Team recommended that the management
direct the Municipal Accountant to:

1. Strictly observe the proper classification and recording of the financial transactions
and adjust the affected liability accounts for its fair presentation in the financial
statements; and,
2. Monitor and verify funds for specific purposes regarding their completion and assess
the subsidiary ledger balances, and coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer in order
to reconcile records and data pertaining to such accounts.

Based on the foregoing observations, the Audit Team recommended that the management
direct the Municipal Accountant to re-visit the propriety of the recording and classification of the
transaction and prepare necessary adjusting entries to correct any errors affecting the faithful
representation of the financial statements.

Based on the foregoing observations, the Audit Team recommended that the management
direct the following officers to:
The Municipal Accountant:
1) Recompute, and verify the amount and details of the Special Trust Fund Account and
make the necessary adjustments and corrections on the account title and the reversion
of the lapse unutilized DRRMF to the surplus of the general fund in accordance with the
provisions of RA 10121 and COA Circular 2012-002; and,
2) Verify the amount and details of the DRRMF and disclose the accurate data in the Notes
to Financial Statement in accordance with the format prescribed in COA Circular 2012-
002;
The Municipal DRRM Officer:
1) Ensure that the PPAs charged from the PY unexpended balances lodged in the Special
Trust Fund are duly indicated in the Annual Investment Plan or any Supplemental
Investment Plan prior to the release and utilization thereof; and,
2) Ensure that the preparation of the LDRRMFIP conforms with the provisions of COA
Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and includes the projects and activities to
be sourced from the previous year’s unexpended balances;
The MPDC:
1) Coordinate with the MDRRMO and prepare the necessary SIPs or revised AIP in
accordance with the provision stated in the DILG-DBM JMC No. 02 dated March 30,
2020.
The Municipal Budget Officer:
1) Periodically reconcile the report pertaining to the utilization and the DRRMF, specifically
the continuing appropriation amounts, with the MDRRMO and the accounting
department in order to ensure the accuracy and completeness of the data as the basis
for the preparation of the financial statements.

Based on the foregoing observations, the Audit Team recommended that the management to:
a. Exert effort in contacting and informing the suppliers/contractors who have claims over
the Guaranty/Security Deposits account, especially those who have already lapsed
beyond the warranty period; and
b. Facilitate the prompt release of the retention money to prevent further accumulation of
balances of the said account.

Based on the foregoing observations, the Audit Team recommended that the management
direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a) Conduct a thorough analysis of the trust fund accounts, determine the dormant accounts
that need to be reverted to the source agency, and coordinate with the implementing
office with regards to the reversion of the unexpended balances;
b) Coordinate with the source agency pertaining to the reversion of the unexpended
balance and/or make a representation for any possible reprogramming of the said
balances.

Based on the foregoing observations, the Audit Team recommended that the management
direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Properly manage the duties and responsibilities assigned in order to have sufficient time,
particularly in maintaining an updated cashbook as prescribed by the pertinent regulations;
b. Ensure that the financial transactions recorded are complete and accurate and should be
reconciled monthly with the records of the accounting department in order to take up
corrections or rectify errors as promptly as possible; and,
c. Avoid or minimize erasures in the cashbook entries in order to raise the assurance of
accuracy and reliability over the recorded transactions.

Based on the foregoing observations, the Audit Team recommended that the management
direct:
The Bids and Awards Committee:
1) Strictly adhere to the stipulations set forth in RA 9184, specifically, embody the declared
policy and the governing principles of government procurement in order to ensure the
utilization of the government funds in an effective and efficient manner;
2) Procurement shall be undertaken based on the submitted APP, and no alterations
should be done unless the APP have been revised in accordance with the guidelines of
Section 7.3 of the Updated Revised IRR of RA 9184;
3) Eliminate discrimination against small contractors/suppliers and encourage
contractors/suppliers to participate in the bidding process in order to support a more
transparent and just procurement process.
The Municipal Engineer:
1) Strictly monitor and supervise projects, especially projects that have overlapping or the
same implementation period, in order to avoid unwarranted delays.

We also recommend that the management direct the Municipal Treasurer to exert effort to notify the payee of the stale checks and advise the same for the cancellation and replacement of the negotiable instrument.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
1. Ensure that the necessary disclosures, as stipulated by IPSAS and GAM for LGU Vol 1, are properly included in the Notes to the Financial Statement;
2. Check the accuracy of the information presented on the face of the Financial Statement, specifically on the presentation of each account, and correct any deficiency noted thereto; and,
3. Trace the error thru manual recalculations and effect the correct balances; and
4. Coordinate with the person in charge of the e-NGAS system and relay any lapses or glitches in the system via online communication or through any mode of communication not needing travel to the central office and face-to-face interaction, in order to correct and prevent any future errors.

We recommend the management to direct the following:
To the Municipal Treasurer:
(a) Maintain a Real Property Tax Register/Taxpayer’s Index Card and update the same based on the assessment roll and the taxes collected yearly;
(b) Manually compute for the RPT and SET Receivable based on the RPT Register/Taxpayer’s Index Card;
(c) Ensure that the amount of the delinquent taxes was incorporated in the established receivable amount to be submitted to the Municipal Accountant; and,
(d) Submit the necessary report to the Accounting Office at the end of the year for the recognition of the receivable at the beginning of the following year.
To the Municipal Accountant:
(e) Ensure that the delinquent taxes and the report submitted by the MTO are reconciled in order to achieve the accurate figure to recognize; and,
(f) Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and set up the RPT and SET Receivable on the first day of the year.

We recommend the Local Chief Executive Officer to direct the Local Finance Committee to ensure that the report submitted with regards to the utilization of the BGCM is accurate and that it coincides with the pertinent documents such as the subsidiary ledger and disbursement vouchers.

We recommend the Local Chief Executive Officer to direct the Municipal Accountant and the Property Officer to analyze the discrepancies between the ledger balance and the RPCPPE, and to make necessary adjustments in their records to take up the noted differences. observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the non-current portion of the Loans Payable – Domestic account and Prior Period Adjustments for better presentation of the accounts in the financial statements. Moreover, provide necessary information relating to the above-cited accounts for inclusion in the Notes to FS.

We recommend the Local Chief Executive to direct the concerned department heads to comply with the accounting and reporting requirements set forth under the COA Circular 2015-008. Moreover, direct the Municipal Accountant to record the regular repairs and maintenance of the road networks as part of expense instead of capitalizing that to infrastructure asset account.

We recommend the Local Chief Executive to direct the concerned department heads to require them to prepare and maintain records such as stock cards for each class of supplies and inventory. At the end of the year, require them to submit to the Office of the Municipal Accountant the said records/reports which will be the basis of adjustments of the ending balance of inventory. Moreover, adhere to the rules and procedures in accounting for supplies and materials provided under the NGAS Manual. All purchases of supplies and materials should be taken up as inventories under the appropriate account and review properly the accounting entries and the account use to avoid committing similar errors.

We recommend the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct a thorough review and analysis of unliquidated obligations or accounts payable which are outstanding for more than 2 years. When determined to have no valid claims, prepare adjusting entries for the reversion of the same to the unappropriated surplus. Further, monitor ageing of accounts payable regularly for proper management and disposition to avoid the accumulation of dormant accounts.

Based on the foregoing observations, we recommend that the management direct the
Municipal Accountant to:
a) Thoroughly reconcile the cash balance as stated in the book with the balance stated in
bank statements, determine valid reconciling items, communicate with the bank for any
error attributable to them, and prepare and record any adjusting or correcting entries
regarding the reconciling items;
b) Ensure that adjusting and correcting entries were made promptly for the valid
reconciling items determined in the BRS;
c) Revert/ restore the cash corresponding to the stale checks and re-establish the payable
account;
d) For the stale checks with no verifiable details, coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer
and trace and verify the same to complete the information and prepare proper adjusting
entries for the reversion of cash and reestablishment of payable accounts; and,
e) Exercise proper diligence in the preparation of adjusting entries, specifically in the
mathematical computation of any figures thereof.
We also recommend that the management direct the Municipal Treasurer to exert effort
to notify the payee of the stale checks and advise the same for the cancellation and
replacement of the negotiable instrument.

In line with the foregoing observations, we recommend the management to direct the
Municipal Accountant the following course of actions:
a) Constantly review and monitor the system-generated depreciation schedule for the PPE
and evaluate the correctness of the data using the underlying provisions and basis of
computation provided by Section 262 of GAM for LGU Vol 1, PAG 3 for IPSAS 17, and
other generally accepted accounting principles (GAAP);
b) Create a report to the Government Accounting Sector (GAS) of the Commission on Audit
(COA) or any person in charge of the e-NGAS system regarding any glitches or flaws on
the implementation of the said application in order to appropriately log the said glitches
and correct or fix the same;
c) Exert effort to create a manual computation on the depreciation of the PPEs as a guide for
the correct figures and adjust accordingly any errors in contrary to the underlying
guidelines;
d) Consider designating a personnel who has knowledge in computer systems for real-time
troubleshooting of the minor glitches of the accounting system;
e) Ensure that all PPEs subject for depreciation have been provided with a depreciation
expense and that all data necessary have been appropriately logged in, such as the
acquisition cost in compliance with IPSAS 17 and other pertinent accounting regulations,
and to recompute or create a new lapsing schedule for the depreciation following the
adjusted details on useful life and acquisition cost;
f) Install an internal control with regards to counter-checking the details logged in the
system and ensure that complete and accurate data have been provided;
g) Recompute the depreciation based on the change in accounting estimate due to the
alteration of the residual value as stipulated in the GAM for LGU Vol. 1 and apply the
same in prospective application on the period following the audit year;
h) Prepare the appropriate journal entries and other accounting records to correct the
depreciation and effect the necessary adjustments in order to rectify the errors and present
the asset accounts to their proper carrying amount; and,
i) Exert effort to properly identify and verify the existence of the PPEs that have already
lapsed in useful life in order to confirm its existence and reassess any estimates provided
for such accounts.

Based on the foregoing observations, we recommend the management to direct the
ensuing personnels the enumerated course of actions:
To the Municipal Accountant:
1) Exert effort to manually monitor the system-generated PPE ledgers and reconcile the
same with the RPCPPE submitted by the GSO;
2) Compute or trace any discrepancy with the RPCPPE and prepare proper adjusting entries
to correct the same; and,
3) Ensure that the accounting procedures were done in a timely manner, through proper time
management and sound internal control, in order to achieve a fair presentation of
financial statements.
To the Municipal General Service Officer:
4) Maintain proper and complete records of the PPEs, particularly the stock cards and
property cards as stipulated under the Government Accounting Manual for LGU Vol 1;
5) Determine unserviceable PPEs and conduct proper procedures to dispose of such
accounts, in coordination with the accounting department to derecognize the same in the
books of the agency; and,
6) Ensure that any information included in the report presents fair and accurate data that is
reliable and verifiable against supporting documents.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant the
following course of actions:
1. Ensure that the necessary disclosures, as stipulated by IPSAS and GAM for LGU
Vol 1, are properly included in the Notes to the Financial Statement;
2. Check the accuracy of the information presented on the face of the Financial
Statement, specifically on the presentation of each account, and correct any
deficiency noted thereto; and,
3. Trace the error thru manual recalculations and effect the correct balances; and
4. Coordinate with the person in charge of the e-NGAS system and relay any lapses
or glitches in the system via online communication or through any mode of
communication not needing travel to the central office and face-to-face
interaction, in order to correct and prevent any future errors.

Based on the foregoing, we recommend the management to direct the following course of
actions:
To the Municipal Treasurer:
a) Maintain a Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s Index Card and update
the same based on the assessment roll and the taxes collected yearly;
b) Manually compute for the RPT and SET Receivable based on the RPT Register/
Taxpayer’s Index Card;
c) Ensure that the amount of the delinquent taxes was incorporated in the established
receivable amount to be submitted to the Municipal Accountant;
d) Submit the necessary report to the Accounting Office at the end of the year for the
recognition of the receivable at the beginning of the following year.
To the Municipal Accountant:
e) Ensure that the delinquent taxes and the report submitted by the MTO are reconciled
in order to achieve the accurate figure to recognize; and,
f) Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and set up the RPT and SET
Receivable on the first day of the year.

Based on the foregoing, we recommend that the management direct the BAC the
following course of actions:
1) Communicate with the GPPB and inquire regarding any error on the posting
requirements and posting entries of the required information; inquire for the reason
why the submitted entries were not posted;
2) Exert effort to coordinate with the end-users or implementing departments after the
approval of the appropriation ordinance for any changes in the APP and update and/or
revise the same;
3) Ensure that the details in the APP, particularly the ABC, is consistent with
appropriations for the project authorized in the GAA, continuing, and automatic
appropriations, the corporate budget, and the appropriations ordinance, as the case
may be;
4) Ensure that any updated reports or revised reports are submitted to the Commission
on Audit at least 5 days after the change has been reflected and approved for
auditorial review;
5) Prepare the project requirements and attach them with the contracts and DVs
submitted in order to support the validity, verifiability, and completeness of the
claims; and,
6) Properly check the completeness of the documentary requirements submitted by each
supplier/ contractor in each contract/ transaction undertaken with the agency.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive Officer, as head of the agency
to:
1) Require the Accountant and the Property Officer to maintain complete
property and equipment ledger cards as required under Sec. 43 of NGAS,
Volume I & Sec C. 3, Chapter V of the Manual on Property Custodianship;
2) Require the Inventory Committee to conduct year-end physical inventory on
Property, Plant & Equipment, including the Construction-in-Progress, Road
Networks and completed Infrastructure Projects and submit it to the
Accounting for reconciliation of balances as a requirement to Sec 57 of the
NGAS Manual, Volume I to ensure correctness and reliability of the PPE
account balances;
3) Require the Accountant and the General Services Officer to analyze the
discrepancy between the balances in the books and per physical count of the
PPE Accounts and to make the necessary adjustments in their records to
take up the noted differences affecting current year’s transactions. Also,
locate the missing/unreconciled properties;
4) Require the GSO to annually provide a copy of the reconciled RCPPE to the
COA, not later than the 31st day of January of ensuing year, showing therein
the proper classification of PPE as shown in PPSAS accounts. Also, prepare
Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Treasurer to submit, every beginning of the year, to the Municipal Accountant a duly
certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the
year pursuant to Section 20 of the NGAS Manual to reconcile the variance noted and
avoid the improper set-up of RPT/SET Receivable in the succeeding periods.

Recommendations:
We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to direct the department heads
involved to:
a. Adhere strictly with the rules and regulations for the granting, utilization
and liquidation of cash advances as prescribed under Section 89 of PD 1445
and COA Circular 97-002.
b. Send demand letters to the concerned employees. If no settlement or
satisfactory explanation is received within thirty (30) days, cause the
withholding of salary due to the accountable officer/s.
c. Refrain from granting additional cash advances to accountable officers who
have previous unsettled cash advances.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the local government unit discontinue the practice of
allocating lump-sum, discretionary funds to the members of Sangguniang Bayan in its
Annual Budget.

Recommendations:
We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to direct the department heads
involved to:
a. Adhere strictly with the rules and regulations for the granting of cash
advances as prescribed under COA Circular 97-002.
b. Refrain from granting cash advances which are not in the scope of the
existing laws, rules and regulations.

Recommendations:
We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to direct the department heads
involved to:
a. Closely monitor the utilization and implementation of priority projects for
the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental
outcomes and to maximize the usage of the fund;
b. Fast track the implementation based on the schedule time frame by the
implementing department concerned to achieve the desired management
outcomes;
c. Determine the reason for non-implementation of some projects and evaluate
the possibility to make amendments to some projects which are not feasible
for implementation;
d. Strict compliance with the provisions of JMC 2017-1 of DILG and DBM
should be observed.

Recommendation:
We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal
Finance Committee to include in annual appropriation the amount necessary to cover
the premiums for the insurance of its properties and faithfully comply with the
guidelines set forth by the Commission on Audit as prescribed in the COA Circular
2018-002 dated May 31, 2018.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal
Treasurer and other accountable officers to perform their responsibilities faithfully by
depositing immediately and intact all collections accruing to the LGU with the
government depository bank and that the unwarranted failure to fulfill these
responsibilities deserves administrative sanctions. Moreover, the required Report of
Accountability for Accountable Forms should be prepared and submitted to the audit
team every month.

Recommendation:
We recommended for the management to ensure that Journal Entry Vouchers
be properly signed by the preparer and the authorized approving officer as required in
the prescribed form to ensure the authenticity and reliability of the accounting record.
Also, direct the Municipal Accountant to sign the Journal Entry Vouchers for the
Calendar 2018 and onwards.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the LGU be sensitized to the gains from making use of
the available remedies provided for in the Code to enforce the collection of the property
taxes, giving more focus on the delinquent receivables. Also, intensify its strategies
and/or utilize other feasible collection approaches to prevent the accumulation of past
due accounts.

Bungubong last April 27, 2015 and was endorsed to LGU Alamada on May 11, 2015. In the succeeding years, the GPBs were submitted to concern agencies before its implementation.

observations.recommendation: Recommended that the municipal government, during the preparation of its GAD Accomplishment Report, administer the HGDG test on its GAD PPAs for CY 2015 as basis in determining the actual expenditure that can be attributed to the GAD budget pursuant to Item 4.1.C.4.5.e of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: The programs and projects included in the GAD 2105 was an output of our workshop. Rest assured that in the succeeding years we will use the HGDG tool in the identification of GAD program

observations.recommendation: The municipal government should create its GAD M&E Team or expand the functions and membership of the Municipal Project Monitoring Committee to include the GFPS TWG Chair, Human Resource Management Officer, Budget Officer, representative/s of the LCE, civil society organizations representative/s specifically from women’s organizations and the academe pursuant to Item 5 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: The LCE issued Executive Order No 015 stating the composition of the GAD monitoring and evaluation team to include the HRMO, Budget Officer, representative of the LCE and civil society organization  presidents from different women’s group in the municipality

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasury Office to furnish the Municipal Accountant with the certified list of taxpayers as basis in establishing the RPT Receivable.
observations.managementAction:
As per recommendation that requires to furnish the Municipal Accountant certified list of tax payers as basis for RPT receivables, we are informing that we have
already submitted to the Municipal  Accountant for compliance.

observations.recommendation: The management should conduct yearly physical inventory of the agency’s properties and provide for depreciation in accordance with existing regulation.
observations.managementAction: Conduct inventory including buildings, land and other structure owned by the LGU which are recorded in the accounting office.
observations.reason: We actually submitted yearly inventory report for LGU properties, plant and equipments. But  the problem was we only focused on equipments and furniture. We forget to include the inventory for building, land and other structure.

observations.recommendation: The Local Executive Officer require the General Services Officer or any authorized official to regularly submit the Monthly Report of Official Travels with accomplished Drivers’ Trip Ticket for each vehicle/equipment used, including the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption to the Office of the Auditor for verification, monitoring and auditing of fuel, oil and lubricants used in accordance with COA Circular 77-61.
observations.managementAction: We will collect and record all necessary  supporting documents in the payment of fuel and lubricants such as travel order, trip tickets, and summary of consumption. We will also require all offices to submit their monthly fuel consumption for consolidation.
observations.reason: The documents for  the payment of fuel and lubricants were already submitted with trip tickets and summary of fuel consumption. We have no travel order and failed the consolidate.

observations.recommendation: The Management shall see to it that the minimum allowable deduction from payroll shall not result to a net take home pay of below P3,000.00.  Those personnel whom have loan agreement with private lending institutions must directly remit their payments to the collecting agent/s or office of respective lending establishments.
observations.managementAction: Enforce the mandatory net pay of P3,000.00

observations.recommendation: The management require the Municipal’s Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report to be approved by the head of the procuring entity and submit the same to the Government Procurement and Policy Board (GPPB) in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester. Also, make use of this report as a tool in monitoring, assessing and improving the procurement processes.
observations.managementAction: Preparation of procurement monitoring report and submission to concerned agency within the required period.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the BAC to be resolute of its responsibility in ensuring that the LGU abides by the standards set forth in the government procurement law.  It may create an internal control to make sure all provisions of RA 9184.are adhered to.
observations.managementAction: Posting of all projects/ programs is the Philgeps website

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the local chief executive to:

Resort to NFA as primary supplier or source of rice in all its procurements whenever it grants rice as a form of incentive or benefit to their employees or use rice in connection with their functions; and

Forge with the NFA an appropriate Memorandum of Agreement to facilitate and operationalize the rice procurement arrangement.
observations.managementAction: Procure rice from NFA outlets

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the local government unit, through the chief executive to:

Revisit, review and evaluate the programs/projects/activities included in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan of the Municipality to ensure effective formulation, implementation and utilization of LDRRM Fund; and 

Ensure strict utilization of LDRRM funds for calamity-related activities so as to accomplish all planned projects and activities.
observations.managementAction: Comply with existing guideline

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the local chief executive to evaluate the necessity of funding and hire of Medical Technologist to undergo the requirements on physical and medical examination to ensure public health safety and assure of additional income in imposing fees for the purpose.
observations.managementAction: With med tech hired from the Regional office already

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the local chief executive to secure copies of PNDF Volume 1, current edition, and to direct the Municipal Health Officer and other requisitioning officers to refer as basis for procurements of drugs the PNDF and thereby, supplement certification for each request the purchase thereof.
